Source_Species.reason: The study concerns Arabidopsis mutants/transgenics; experimental procedures state Arabidopsis thaliana materials.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Explicitly stated throughout, including Keywords and Plant materials: Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Both Col and Ler backgrounds are explicitly used: hmc1 and RNAi in Col; laf6 and complementation in Ler.

Applied_Species.reason: All functional tests were performed in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Both Col and Ler backgrounds were experimentally analyzed.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Title and summary describe SUFB as an iron–sulfur cluster biosynthesis protein; results/discussion place it in the SUFBCD complex.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show SUFB deficiency reduces chlorophyll and causes Mg-proto MME accumulation consistent with impaired Mg-proto MME cyclase activity; far-red long-hypocotyl phenotype of laf6 was not due to SUFB.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is directly supported in Results: pale-green/reduced chlorophyll, Mg-proto MME accumulation, white-light complementation of laf6 by SUFB overexpression, and lack of rescue of laf6 FR/dark phenotypes.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper directly measures chlorophyll a and b reduction and frames SUFB as required for chlorophyll synthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Chlorophyll is a tetrapyrrole pigment; this is consistent with the paper's tetrapyrrole/chlorophyll pathway context.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Chlorophyll and intermediates were assayed in developing leaves of 4-week-old plants and 7-day-old seedlings.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Chlorophyll's role in photosynthetic light harvesting is general knowledge, not directly stated/tested in the Results of this paper.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The paper used hmc1 and laf6 mutants, Dex-inducible RNAi, 35S::SUFB complementation/overexpression, HPLC pigment profiling, and immunoblotting.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Experimental procedures explicitly note RNAi suppression effects were visible only in developing leaves of mature plants; assays also included 7-day-old seedlings.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The paper repeatedly frames SUFB in chloroplast Fe–S cluster biosynthesis and lists 'chloroplast' as a keyword; localization is supported at paper level.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Results explicitly discuss SUFB as part of the SUFBCD complex and compare SUFC and SUFD deficiency phenotypes.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Experiments were performed under long-day white light, darkness/etiolation, far-red light, and dexamethasone-induced RNAi conditions.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Results and methods state a Dex-inducible promoter was used for RNAi and 35S::SUFB was used for complementation.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Strong depletion causing pale-green/reduced chlorophyll is directly shown; embryo lethality of knockout lines is explicitly stated as the reason knockouts were unavailable.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an extrapolative application claim not directly evaluated in the paper, though the deleterious effect of loss and embryo lethality are supported.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results show laf6 long-hypocotyl segregates independently from low chlorophyll and that SUFC/SUFD phenocopy Mg-proto MME accumulation, supporting a SUFBCD-complex rather than SUFB-specific effect.

Source_Species.reason: SUFC RNAi constructs were made from Arabidopsis gene sequence and tested in Arabidopsis plants.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Plant materials and cloning are in Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: SUFC RNAi lines were analyzed alongside Col background plants; Figure 3 uses WT/transgenics in this context.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The SUFC RNAi work was done in the Col background.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: SUFC is stated to be part of the SUFBCD complex, but the specific label 'ATPase subunit' is not given in this paper text.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state SUFC RNAi lines showed pale-green leaves and accumulated Mg-proto MME similar to SUFB-deficient lines, indicating requirement for chlorophyll synthesis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three listed findings are stated in the Results section around Figure 3.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper links SUFC deficiency to impaired chlorophyll synthesis via pale-green phenotype and chlorophyll-pathway intermediate accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Chlorophyll is treated in the paper as part of the tetrapyrrole biosynthetic pathway.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Figure 3 specifies pigments extracted from young leaves after Dex treatment.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Photosynthetic light harvesting/plant growth is not a specific result claim for SUFC in this paper.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: SUFC was tested by conditional Dex-inducible RNAi, phenotype observation, and HPLC pigment profiling.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: While SUFC is discussed as part of chloroplast SUF machinery by context, this paper does not directly state SUFC localization.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: SUFC is explicitly analyzed as part of the SUFBCD complex with SUFB and SUFD.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: SUFC RNAi phenotype was assessed after dexamethasone treatment in long-day-grown plants.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods describe Dex-inducible RNAi constructs for SUFC.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Knockdown caused pale-green phenotype, directly shown in Figure 3 and text.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an inferred application statement, not directly tested or discussed as an engineering target in the paper.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly uses SUFC RNAi mainly to show that Mg-proto MME accumulation reflects impaired SUFBCD complex/Fe–S cluster synthesis rather than a unique SUFB function.

Source_Species.reason: SUFD RNAi constructs were generated from Arabidopsis sequences and tested in Arabidopsis.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The experimental system is Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: SUFD RNAi lines were analyzed in the Col background context.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The SUFD RNAi analyses were performed with Col-background WT/transgenics.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Membership in the SUFBCD complex is supported, but the specific descriptor 'scaffold subunit' is not stated in this paper.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state SUFD RNAi lines are pale green and accumulate Mg-proto MME, supporting a requirement in chlorophyll synthesis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: These exact findings are reported in the Results around Figure 3.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper links SUFD deficiency to impaired chlorophyll synthesis via phenotype and chlorophyll intermediate profiling.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Chlorophyll is part of the tetrapyrrole pigment pathway addressed in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Figure 3 analyzed pigments from young leaves after Dex treatment.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The broad functional statement is not directly established in Results for SUFD.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: SUFD was directly tested using Dex-inducible RNAi, visible phenotype analysis, and HPLC pigment profiling.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The paper does not explicitly state SUFD localization, though chloroplast context is implied.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: SUFD is explicitly discussed as a component of the SUFBCD complex with SUFB and SUFC.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The SUFD RNAi phenotype was analyzed after dexamethasone induction under long-day conditions.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state Dex-inducible RNAi constructs were used for SUFD.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Knockdown caused a pale-green chlorophyll-deficient phenotype in the reported experiments.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an inferred application claim not directly supported by paper data.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly includes SUFD to demonstrate that impaired Fe–S cluster synthesis by the SUFBCD complex causes Mg-proto MME accumulation.
